---
title: "Connected Data Products Hackdays Overview"
date: "2026-05-13"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default

author:
  - name: "{{< var people.trey.name >}}"
    orcid: "{{< var people.trey.orcid >}}"
  - name: "{{< var people.robyn.name >}}"
    orcid: "{{< var people.robyn.orcid >}}"
  - name: "Luis López "
    orcid: "0000-0003-4896-3263"
---


## Connected Data Products Hackdays

* Berkeley, CA April 13-15
* Co-hosted by [Englacial](https://englacial.org/) and [Berkeley Institute for Data
  Science](https://bids.berkeley.edu/).
* Focus on bringing together members of the cryospheric data science community
  to explore connected and living data products.
* April 13: Presentations/demos and ideas formation
* April 14-15: Hack time!

::: {.notes}
Englacial is funeded by [Astera](https://astera.org/), which provided support
for this event. Englacial focuses on connecting observational data to numerical
models of ice sheets.

Astera is a scientific technology company with a strong focus on AI.
:::

## The group


![Image courtesy of
Englacial](https://englacial.org/posts/20240428-hackdays-projects/images/hackdays-participants.jpg){fig-align="center"}

::: {.notes}
~30 participants (25 pictured) from the cryospheric data science community.
:::

## Demos/Presentations

::: {.r-fit-text}
* Open Polar Radar Tools ([xopr](https://github.com/englacial/xopr)): Python
  package for accessing and analyzing [CReSIS](https://cresis.ku.edu/) radar
  depth sounder data.
* [icepyx](https://github.com/icesat2py/icepyx): Python library for searching
  and accessing IceSat2 data.
* [GStatSim](https://github.com/GatorGlaciology/GStatSim): Python package for
  geostatistical interpolation methods.
* [NISAR](https://science.nasa.gov/mission/nisar/) data at [ASF
  DAAC](https://asf.alaska.edu/).
* [Arcadia](https://research.arcadiascience.com/) bioscience overview and demo
  of [The Stacks](https://thestacks.org/) scientific publishing platform.
* [MyST](https://mystmd.org/) for scientific publishing with markdown.
* [Source Cooperative](https://source.coop/): nonprofit publishing of scientific
  data to cloud infrastructure.
* [Cloud-Native Geospatial Forum](https://cloudnativegeo.org/) overview and
  [data formats guide](https://guide.cloudnativegeo.org/)
* [Cryocloud](https://cryointhecloud.com/): Collaborative Earth Science Platform
  in the cloud.
* [VirtualiZarr](https://virtualizarr.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html) and
  [IceChunk](https://icechunk.io/en/stable/).
* [ISMIP 6 Model Viewer](https://englacial.org/models.html)
:::

## Hack projects

::: {.r-fit-text}
* [earthaccess](https://earthaccess.readthedocs.io/) v1.0 updates
* Virtualization of datasets for efficient cloud access (e.g,. radar sounder data).
* Analysis of velocity changes at grounding zones.
* Analysis of radar echo sounding crossovers with IceSat2 crossovers.
* Geostatistics/Interpolation of data with
  [GStatSim](https://github.com/GatorGlaciology/GStatSim).

Englacial posted a nice writeup of the hackdays projects here:

<https://englacial.org/posts/20240428-hackdays-projects/index.html>!
:::

## earthaccess v1 hacking

::: {.r-fit-text}
* Trey, Joe Kennedy (ASF), Julia Lober (graduate student at Boise State), Matt
  Fisher (DSE), and Jessica Scheick (U. of New Hampshire)
* The project is working toward a v1 release, and there are various
  milestones/breaking changes that we want to complete before v1 is done.
* We ended up focusing on updates to the results API, and providing convenience
  methods/properties to the `Results` class (e.g., we added the ability to
  export to GeoDataframe and worked toward attaching query provenance to the
  results).
* Associated PR: <https://github.com/earthaccess-dev/earthaccess/pull/1298>
* Lots of people expressed interest in this work, and were excited for the
  changes. Almost all of the projects (maybe all) used earthaccess.
:::

## GStatSim: New Applications for Large Radar Datasets

::: {.r-fit-text}
* **Team:** Robyn Marowitz, Eliza Dawson (NOAA C&GC Postdoc Fellow, Georgia Tech),
  Joseph Rotondo (UW grad student), Lindsay Stark (Boise State grad student), Emmy Muniz (Stanford undergraduate student)
* Expanded the [GStatSim](https://gatorglaciology.github.io/gstatsimbook/intro.html)
  Jupyter Book documentation with two new real-world application notebooks:
  * **HiCARS radar sounder** — end-to-end workflow: downloading 860 granules from
    NSIDC via [`earthaccess`](https://earthaccess.readthedocs.io/), parsing flight-line
    text files into a single [GeoParquet](https://geoparquet.org/) (9.3M points,
    EPSG:3031), and fitting variogram models for kriging and geostatistical simulation
  * **Arctic pole hole** — filling the gap in satellite sea-ice coverage near the
    pole using geostatistical interpolation of sea-ice albedo
* Together these notebooks demonstrate real-world approaches to connecting datasets
  through an open-source package to produce a new data product
* Repo: <https://github.com/elizadawson/gstatsim_largedatasets>
:::

## Common themes

::: {.r-fit-text}
* Data archived in the cloud is not always "cloud-optimized" or
  "AI-ready". Significant interest in data virtualization techniques (e.g,
  [VirtualiZarr](https://virtualizarr.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html),
  [IceChunk](https://icechunk.io/en/stable/))
* Importance of living/executable documentation (e.g,. via
  [MyST](https://mystmd.org/)).
* Discussions about how to maintain citations for products that receive updates
  from a rotating cast of contributors.
* The role of AI in scientific software/product development (e.g., [Jupyter
  AI](https://jupyter-ai.readthedocs.io/en/v3/)).
* Reproducibility in science is more than just being open. Can AI be used as a
  "reproducibility checker"?
* Challenges with scaling notebooks to production
* Challenges with co-locating and co-analyzing data that were not "designed" to
  work together natively.
:::


## Questions?
